content.problemStatement:
Chef has a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. He wants to sort it in non-decreasing order. Unfortunately, he can only modify it using specific operations. In each operation, Chef should do the following:


Choose an integer KKK such that 0<K<2300 \lt K \lt 2^{30}0<K<230.
Choose an integer PPP such that 1≤P≤N1 \leq P \leq N1≤P≤N.
Perform one of the following actions:

type t=1t = 1t=1: for each iii (1≤i≤P1 \le i \le P1≤i≤P), change AiA_iAi​ to Ai+KA_i+KAi​+K
type t=2t = 2t=2: for each iii (P≤i≤NP \le i \le NP≤i≤N), change AiA_iAi​ to Ai+KA_i+KAi​+K
type t=3t = 3t=3: for each iii (1≤i≤P1 \le i \le P1≤i≤P), change AiA_iAi​ to Ai⊕KA_i \oplus KAi​⊕K
type t=4t = 4t=4: for each iii (P≤i≤NP \le i \le NP≤i≤N), change AiA_iAi​ to Ai⊕KA_i \oplus KAi​⊕K


Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR. In different operations, Chef may choose different values of ttt, KKK and PPP.

Can you help Chef make the sequence non-decreasing using at most ⌊N/2⌋\lfloor N/2 \rfloor⌊N/2⌋ operations (possibly 000 times)? Find one such sequence of operations.

Note that you do not need to minimise the number of operations. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them. It is guaranteed that a solution using at most ⌊N/2⌋\lfloor N/2 \rfloor⌊N/2⌋ operations always exists.
constraints: the sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases does not exceed 10710^7107
`Explanation**Example case 1:** After changing A2A_2A2​ to 10⊕1110 \oplus 1110⊕11 and A3A_3A3​ to 9⊕119 \oplus 119⊕11, the sequence becomes [1,1,2][1, 1, 2][1,1,2], which is non-decreasing.

**Example case 2:** In the first operation, we change A4A_4A4​ to 1⊕41 \oplus 41⊕4 and the sequence becomes [4,3,5,5][4, 3, 5, 5][4,3,5,5]. Then, we add 222 to A2A_2A2​, A3A_3A3​ and A4A_4A4​. The sequence becomes [4,5,7,7][4, 5, 7, 7][4,5,7,7], which is non-decreasing.

**Example case 3:** After the first operation, the sequence AAA becomes [0,1,3,3,7,5][0, 1, 3, 3, 7, 5][0,1,3,3,7,5]. After the second operation, the sequence becomes [0,1,1,1,5,7][0, 1, 1, 1, 5, 7][0,1,1,1,5,7], which is non-decreasing.
**Example case 4:** After performing the only operation, the sequence becomes [4,5,6][4, 5, 6][4,5,6], which is non-decreasing.
**Example case 5:** The sequence [1,2,3][1, 2, 3][1,2,3] is already non-decreasing, so we do not need to perform any operations.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


First, print a line containing a single integer XXX ― the number of operations you want to perform (0≤X≤⌊N/2⌋0 \leq X \leq \lfloor N/2 \rfloor0≤X≤⌊N/2⌋).
Then, print XXX lines. Each of these lines should contain three space-separated integers ttt, PPP and KKK describing an operation (1≤t≤41 \leq t \leq 41≤t≤4).
